annotations.label: Finitary relation

anchor.snippet: a finitary relation over a sequence of sets

annotations.note: Mathlib has the binary case via `SetRel α β := Set (α × β)`, but no general n-ary relation type over an arbitrary sequence of sets.

annotations.label: Arity of a relation

anchor.snippet: is called the arity

annotations.note: No notion of arity attached to a relation appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of a relation

anchor.snippet: called the graph of

annotations.note: `Function.graph` gives the graph of a function as a `SetRel`; the more general 'graph of a relation' (the underlying set in the n-ary case) is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: R-related notation

anchor.snippet: is read " x 1 , ..., x n are R -related"

annotations.note: For the binary case Mathlib provides the scoped notation `a ~[R] b` for `(a,b) ∈ R`; no n-ary analogue exists.

annotations.label: ith domain of R

anchor.snippet: is called the i th domain of

annotations.note: No notion of the i-th domain of an n-ary relation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonsimple domain

anchor.snippet: is called a nonsimple domain of

annotations.note: The 'nonsimple domain' terminology is database-theoretic and not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ith domain of definition (active domain)

anchor.snippet: is called the i th domain of definition or active domain of

annotations.note: For binary relations, `SetRel.dom`/`SetRel.cod` give the active domain/codomain (projections of the support); no n-ary version exists.

annotations.label: Total on ith domain

anchor.snippet: is said to be total on its i th domain

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Relator.LeftTotal` and `Relator.RightTotal` for binary relations; no general 'total on i-th domain' for n-ary.

annotations.label: Left-total / serial / right-total / surjective

anchor.snippet: it is also said to be left-total

annotations.note: `Relator.LeftTotal` (`∀ a, ∃ b, R a b`) and `Relator.RightTotal` (`∀ b, ∃ a, R a b`) match the binary notions exactly.

annotations.label: Unique on a set; primary key

anchor.snippet: is said to be unique on

annotations.note: The relational 'unique on a set'/primary-key terminology is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left-unique (injective) / univalent (right-unique)

anchor.snippet: it is also said to be left-unique or injective

annotations.note: `Relator.LeftUnique` and `Relator.RightUnique` match the binary left-/right-unique definitions exactly.

annotations.label: Homogeneous vs heterogeneous relation

anchor.snippet: called a homogeneous relation

annotations.note: Mathlib distinguishes `SetRel α α` from `SetRel α β` implicitly through types, but does not name the homogeneous/heterogeneous dichotomy.

annotations.label: Empty relation over empty domain

anchor.snippet: the only relation over such a sequence of domains is the empty relation

annotations.note: No specific declaration about the empty n-ary relation over an empty sequence of domains.

annotations.label: Characteristic function of R

anchor.snippet: The characteristic function of

annotations.note: Mathlib treats relations as `Prop`-valued functions natively; `Set.indicator` provides general characteristic functions but not as a notion tied to relations specifically.

annotations.label: n-ary predicate

anchor.snippet: it is common to refer to a Boolean-valued function as an

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `α → β → ... → Prop` directly as n-ary predicates; there is no named 'n-ary predicate' wrapper.

annotations.label: Definition of n-ary relation (theorem box)

anchor.value: Definition — An n -ary relation R on set

annotations.note: No bundled n-ary relation type indexed by `Fin n → Type*` carrying a subset of the product is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nullary relations

anchor.snippet: Nullary (0-ary) relations count only two members

annotations.note: Nullary relations as a distinguished concept are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unary relations

anchor.snippet: Unary (1-ary) relations can be viewed as a collection of members

annotations.note: Unary relations correspond to `Set α` (or `α → Prop`), which Mathlib uses throughout, but they are not labelled 'unary relations'.

annotations.label: Nullary function is a unary relation

anchor.snippet: Every nullary function is a unary relation

annotations.note: This informal observation is not stated as a lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary relations

anchor.snippet: Binary (2-ary) relations are the most commonly studied form

annotations.note: `SetRel α β := Set (α × β)` formalizes binary relations exactly, with extensive supporting API.

annotations.label: Equality and inequality

anchor.snippet: Equality and inequality

annotations.note: Equality `Eq` (and inequality `Ne`) are pervasive core/Mathlib binary relations.

annotations.label: Divisibility

anchor.snippet: Divisibility , denoted by the sign

annotations.note: The `Dvd` typeclass and its `∣` notation provide the divisibility relation throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Set membership

anchor.snippet: Set membership , denoted by the sign

annotations.note: The `Membership` typeclass with `∈` notation provides set membership as a binary relation.

annotations.label: Ternary relations

anchor.snippet: Ternary (3-ary) relations include

annotations.note: Ternary relations are written ad hoc as `α → β → γ → Prop`; no dedicated 'ternary relation' definition exists.

annotations.label: Ternary relation 'x thinks that y likes z'

anchor.snippet: Consider the ternary relation

annotations.note: This illustrative example is not present in Mathlib.
